Sand Cat — the only cat species that lives exclusively in desert environments
Nubian Ibex — a wild mountain goat with huge curved horns found in Jordan's cliffs
sportsAchievements: Ahmad Abughaush won Jordan's first-ever Olympic medal — a gold in taekwondo at the 2016 Rio Olympics
Jordan's basketball team has become one of the strongest in Asia
history: Petra was built by the Nabataeans over 2,000 years ago and was lost to the Western world until 1812.
The Jordan River is one of the most historically significant rivers in the world, important to Judaism, Christianity, and Islam.
Jordan is home to some of the oldest human settlements ever discovered, dating back over 9,000 years.
The ancient Roman city of Jerash in Jordan is one of the best-preserved Roman cities outside of Italy.
trivia: Petra's buildings were carved directly into cliff faces — they didn't stack stones, they carved into the mountain!
You literally cannot sink in the Dead Sea because the water is almost 10 times saltier than the ocean.
Parts of the movie 'Indiana Jones and the Last Crusade' and 'The Martian' were filmed in Jordan.

belgium.name: Belgium
belgium.flagDescription: Three vertical stripes — black (left), yellow (middle), red (right)
belgium.famousFor: Chocolate, waffles, being the headquarters of the European Union, Tintin, and diamond trading
landmarks: Grand Place in Brussels — one of the most beautiful town squares in the world
Manneken Pis — a tiny bronze statue of a little boy, a famous symbol of Brussels
Atomium — a giant building shaped like an iron atom, built for the 1958 World's Fair

geography.longestRiver: Meuse River (Belgian portion) — 118 miles
geography.tallestPeak: Signal de Botrange — 2,277 feet

famousFood: Belgian Waffles — thick, fluffy waffles with deep pockets, topped with chocolate, cream, or fruit
Belgian Chocolate — Belgium produces over 220,000 tons of chocolate per year
Moules-frites — mussels served with crispy french fries (which were actually invented in Belgium!)
festivals: Carnival of Binche — a UNESCO-recognized festival where performers called 'Gilles' wear wax masks and throw oranges
Tomorrowland — one of the world's largest and most famous electronic music festivals, held near Antwerp
famousPeople: Hergé — creator of Tintin, one of the most popular comic book characters in the world
Audrey Hepburn — legendary Hollywood actress born in Brussels
Adolphe Sax — inventor of the saxophone
inventions: The saxophone — invented by Belgian Adolphe Sax in the 1840s
French fries — despite the name, fries were likely invented in Belgium!
The Body Mass Index (BMI) — created by Belgian mathematician Adolphe Quetelet
Praline chocolate — invented in Brussels in 1912
worldRecords: Belgium has the most chocolate shops per square mile in the world
Belgium holds the world record for the longest time without an elected government — 589 days (2010-2011)!
weirdLaws: Every citizen of Belgium over the age of 18 is required by law to vote in elections — you can get fined for not voting!
uniqueAnimals: Belgian Blue Cattle — a breed of cow known for its incredibly muscular appearance
European Hedgehog — commonly found in Belgian gardens
sportsAchievements: Belgium's national football team reached #1 in the FIFA world rankings and finished 3rd at the 2018 World Cup
Eddy Merckx is considered the greatest cyclist of all time, winning the Tour de France 5 times
history: Belgium became independent from the Netherlands in 1830.
Brussels is the unofficial capital of the European Union — many EU institutions are headquartered there.
Belgium has three official languages because it has three distinct cultural regions: Dutch-speaking Flanders, French-speaking Wallonia, and a small German-speaking community.
Antwerp has been the diamond capital of the world for over 500 years — about 80% of the world's rough diamonds pass through the city.
trivia: French fries are actually Belgian! Belgians have been frying potatoes since the 1600s.
Belgium has over 2,000 chocolate shops and produces 220,000 tons of chocolate per year.
The Smurfs, Tintin, and Lucky Luke are all Belgian comic book creations!

trinidadandtobago.name: Trinidad and Tobago
trinidadandtobago.flagDescription: A red background with a diagonal black stripe bordered by white going from upper left to lower right
trinidadandtobago.famousFor: Carnival, steelpan drums, limbo dance, calypso and soca music
landmarks: Pitch Lake — the largest natural deposit of asphalt in the world
Maracas Bay — one of the most famous beaches in the Caribbean
Caroni Bird Sanctuary — home to thousands of Scarlet Ibis birds

geography.longestRiver: Ortoire River — 31 miles
geography.tallestPeak: El Cerro del Aripo — 3,085 feet

famousFood: Doubles — two soft flatbreads filled with curried chickpeas, the most popular street food
Bake and Shark — fried shark meat in a fried bread roll, a beach favorite
Roti — flatbread wrapped around curried meat or vegetables
festivals: Trinidad Carnival — one of the greatest carnivals in the world, with spectacular costumes, steelpan music, and calypso competitions
Diwali — the Festival of Lights, celebrated with thousands of clay lamps lighting up the countryside
famousPeople: Nicki Minaj — global rap superstar born in Trinidad
Brian Lara — cricket legend who holds the record for the highest individual score in a Test innings (400 not out)
V.S. Naipaul — Nobel Prize-winning author born in Trinidad
inventions: Steelpan (steel drum) — the only acoustic musical instrument invented in the 20th century, made from oil barrels
Limbo dance — the famous 'how low can you go' dance originated in Trinidad
Calypso music — a genre born in Trinidad that influenced music worldwide
Soca music — a fusion of soul and calypso created in Trinidad in the 1970s
worldRecords: Trinidad and Tobago is the birthplace of the steelpan, the only new acoustic instrument invented in the 20th century
Pitch Lake in Trinidad is the largest natural asphalt lake in the world — big enough to cover 100 acres
weirdLaws: In Trinidad, it is illegal to wear camouflage clothing — this is reserved exclusively for military personnel!
uniqueAnimals: Scarlet Ibis — a stunning bright red bird and one of Trinidad's national birds
Leatherback Sea Turtle — the world's largest turtle species nests on Trinidad's beaches
Cocrico — a tropical bird found only on the island of Tobago, and the other national bird
sportsAchievements: Brian Lara scored 400 not out in a single Test cricket innings — the highest individual score in Test cricket history
Trinidad and Tobago qualified for the 2006 FIFA World Cup — the smallest country ever to do so at that time
history: Christopher Columbus landed on Trinidad in 1498 and named it after the three peaks he saw from his ship.
The steelpan was invented in the 1930s when people in Trinidad turned discarded oil barrels into musical instruments.
Trinidad and Tobago gained independence from Britain in 1962.
The islands have a rich mix of cultures from Africa, India, Europe, China, and the Middle East, creating one of the most diverse countries in the Caribbean.
trivia: The steelpan is made from old oil drums — Trinidad's oil industry provided the raw materials for this amazing invention!
Trinidad's Pitch Lake has been producing asphalt for roads around the world, including some streets in New York City.
Limbo dancing started in Trinidad — the tradition was inspired by funeral ceremonies where dancers moved under a bar to symbolize the passage to the afterlife.
